title: ActiveCampaign vs Brevo (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign vs Brevo: pricing, features, automation, and CRM capabilities to find the best email marketing tool for your business.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs Brevo: Detailed Comparison Guide
intro: ActiveCampaign and Brevo are two powerful all-in-one marketing platforms that combine email, CRM, and automation features. Both serve businesses looking to streamline customer communication, but they differ significantly in pricing structure, feature focus, and target audience. This guide helps you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing needs.
choose_a_if: You need advanced automation and sophisticated lead scoring capabilities
Your business prioritizes CRM integration with email marketing
You're willing to invest in a premium solution for ecommerce or B2B use cases
choose_b_if: You want to start with a generous free plan supporting up to 100,000 subscribers
You need multi-channel marketing including WhatsApp and live chat functionality
You're a beginner looking for an affordable, feature-rich entry point to marketing automation

faq.a: ActiveCampaign excels in advanced automation and lead scoring, making it better for sophisticated B2B operations. However, Brevo offers superior value for startups and small businesses with its generous free plan and multi-channel capabilities. The "better" choice depends on your business size, budget, and specific automation needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Brevo?
faq.a: Brevo is significantly cheaper for early-stage businesses, offering a free plan with up to 100,000 subscribers at no cost. ActiveCampaign has no free tier and starts at $15/month. For paid plans, Brevo begins at $25/month while ActiveCampaign starts at $15/month, though feature depth differs at each price point.

faq.q: Can I switch from Brevo to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, switching is possible and both platforms support data migration. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your contacts and campaign history, which can then be imported into ActiveCampaign. However, you may need to manually recreate automation workflows, so plan accordingly for a smooth transition.
